Client tracker preview for Selah Midwifery

This page shows how the embedded screenshots fit together as a working Notion setup for Selah Midwifery: A prenatal-to-postpartum tracker for Lydia's solo homebirth practice — intake, monthly-then-weekly prenatal cadence, labs, on-call readiness, and the day-1/day-3/day-7/2wk/4wk/6wk postpartum sequence in one place instead of phone calls, email, and a third-party biller thread.

What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Public workflow used

personalized midwifery care in the comfort of your own home, By 36 weeks, your payment is required to be paid in full before I go on call for your birth, day 1, day 3, and day 7 right at your home. At 2 weeks, 4 weeks, and 6 weeks, at my office, verification of benefits to my third-party biller

Today at Selah Midwifery Prenatal + birth + postpartum board Giselle K. Record Prenatal + Lab View Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View Setup Map

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • One board covers the full arc: intake, prenatal cadence, labs, 36-week paid-in-full gate, on-call window, and postpartum visits
  • Third-party biller status (VOB submitted, claim filed, reimbursement mailed) tracked per family alongside OHP/Medicaid and private-pay lanes
  • Postpartum rhythm (day 1, 3, 7 home + 2wk, 4wk, 6wk office) and newborn exam items (metabolic screen, CCHD, Vitamin K, pap option) flagged before each visit

1. Today at Selah Midwifery

Today at Selah Midwifery

Active prenatals, families in the on-call window, today's visits, and labs pending review — one snapshot so the day starts without scrolling through texts and the third-party biller inbox.

Open this view

2. Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Every family moves left-to-right: Intake → Prenatal (monthly) → Prenatal (weekly) → On-call (37-42 wk) → Postpartum home (day 1/3/7) → Postpartum office (2/4/6 wk). Nothing has to live in a separate paper chart.

Open this view

3. Giselle K. Record

Giselle K. Record

Per-family page: EDD, care plan, prenatal cadence, labs + scans, billing + payment against the 36-wk gate, homebirth readiness, on-call plan, postpartum plan — what used to live across intake PDFs, phone calls, and the biller thread.

Open this view

4. Prenatal + Lab View

Prenatal + Lab View

Every prenatal touchpoint in one screen: cadence stage, last vitals, 28-wk glucose, GBS, supplementation, anatomy scan, billing gate, birth-kit status — before the next visit and before on-call opens.

Open this view

5. Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window

Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window

This week's visits, the 37-42 wk on-call window for active families, the 36-wk paid-in-full gate, and backup/coverage schedule — all on one timeline so three births a month don't collide.

Open this view

6. Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View

Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View

After every birth: day 1/3/7 home visits, 2/4/6-wk office visits, newborn exam items (metabolic screen, CCHD, PKU, Vitamin K, 6-wk pap offer), and lactation referrals — so the postpartum sequence runs itself.

Open this view

7. Setup Map

Setup Map

Seven-step setup: duplicate the template, paste intake language into per-family fields, import active families, set the monthly → weekly cadence, wire the 36-wk paid-in-full gate, track the third-party biller lane, and pre-load the postpartum + newborn exam sequence.

Open this view
